items.briefing:
Australian organisations should be aware of two key developments today. The federal government has replaced the Essential Eight cybersecurity framework with a new "Essentials" standard, so businesses should review what compliance changes may apply to them. Separately, while overall cybercrime risk for Australians is reportedly easing, pressure is shifting toward small and medium businesses — particularly through ransomware attacks where criminals are now impersonating Interpol to trick small businesses into paying fake fines.

Critical vulnerabilities have also doubled globally according to new research, meaning unpatched systems face greater risk than ever. Apple has updated its patching approach to release fixes faster, so ensure your Apple devices are set to update automatically.

**Practical tip:** Check that automatic updates are enabled on all work devices — phones, laptops, and tablets — so security patches apply without delay.

featured.title: Australia replaces Essential Eight with new Essentials
featured.quote: Australia's cyber baseline shift to cloud-aligned standards directly impacts all regulated businesses nationwide.
